# v2.6.0 — EfficientIP Overhaul & NIOS Calculation Alignment

EfficientIP gains full API token authentication and v2.0 API support. NIOS token calculations are realigned to the Infoblox reference methodology — correct licensing rates, corrected Active IP definition, and accurate zone and DTC attribution.

## New Features

### EfficientIP SOLIDserver
- **API Token authentication** — new auth method using the SOLIDserver SHA3-256 SDS signature scheme (`Authorization: SDS <id>:<sig>` + `X-SDS-TS` header). Select **API Token** in the EfficientIP wizard tab.
- **API v2.0 support** — choose between the legacy REST API (`/rest/`) and the modern v2.0 API (`/api/v2.0/`) in EfficientIP Advanced Options. All 15 DNS, IPAM, and DHCP endpoints route to their correct v2.0 paths.

### NIOS Grid
- **CAA, NAPTR, HTTPS, SVCB record types** — four additional DNS record types now counted as DDI objects. Present in NIOS 8.6+ / 9.x.

## Bug Fixes

### NIOS Grid — Calculation Methodology
- **Token rates corrected** — NIOS Grid objects now use NIOS licensing rates (50 DDI objects/token · 25 active IPs/token · 13 assets/token). Previously used native UDDI rates (25/13/3), underestimating the current NIOS cost by 2×. The Migration Planner correctly switches to native UDDI rates (25/13/3) for members migrating to NIOS-X.
- **Active IP definition corrected** — Active IPs now counted as fixed addresses + unique active DHCP leases only. Host record addresses, network boundary IPs, and static leases are excluded from the Active IP count.
- **Forward zone attribution** — forward zones assigned via `ns_group_forwarding_server` are now correctly attributed to their handling member. Previously only authoritative zones were attributed, leaving members that serve exclusively forward zones with 0 objects.
- **DTC Monitor count** — `idns_pool_monitor` objects now correctly counted as DTC Monitors. Individual health-check template objects excluded — they are definitions, not per-pool instances.

### NIOS Grid — Upload
- **Chrome upload failure** — large backups no longer cause Chrome to report *"Failed to fetch"*. `http.MaxBytesReader` was forcing `Connection: close` on every response; replaced with `io.LimitedReader` and explicit body drain after extraction.
- **Upload speed** — member extraction reduced from 35s to 4s for a 576 MB backup (8.5× faster) by switching from `encoding/xml` to the fast byte-level parser. Unused upload temp files now cleaned up automatically after 2 hours.

### EfficientIP SOLIDserver
- **Legacy endpoint name** — `ip_subnet_list` corrected to `ip_block_subnet_list`, the actual SOLIDserver REST endpoint name.
- **Token auth probe on legacy API** — the authentication probe now hits `/rest/member_list` directly on legacy mode instead of `app/node/list`, which is v2.0-only.
- **IPv6 endpoint handling** — SOLIDserver instances without IPv6 enabled return "unknown service" on IPv6 REST endpoints; these now produce count 0 instead of failing the entire scan.

## Download

| Platform | File |
|----------|------|
| Windows | `uddi-token-calculator_windows_amd64.exe` |
| macOS (Apple Silicon) | `uddi-token-calculator_darwin_arm64` |
| Linux | `uddi-token-calculator_linux_amd64` |
